opinion.h4: SIMMONS v. OSWALD, CHAIRMAN, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 500, Misc. Decided October 21, 1963.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, Paxton Blair, Solicitor General, and Winifred C. Stanley, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
375 U.S. 47 THOMPSON v. MISSOURI. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MISSOURI. No. 551, Misc. Decided October 21, 1963.
The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
